Ekanite Review - Empires & Puzzles Strategy Guide

Ekanite brings a unique twist to the Ninja family: minions that force your enemies to punch themselves in the face. But with stats from 2023, can this green ninja still dodge the retirement home? We analyze his charge mechanics, that pesky undispellable counterattack, and whether he's worth your precious tonics today.
Ekanite: The Shinobi Squad Leader Who Hits Back
Ekanite: The Green Ninja Who Brings His Own Bodyguards

Ekanite is a 5-star Nature (Green) hero from the Ninja Tower family, released back in early 2023. He operates on the unique "Charge" mana speed mechanic, allowing him to fire at x1, x2, or x3 mana. While many Ninjas are selfish damage dealers, Ekanite plays the role of a squad leader, bringing minions to the party that grant a unique, undispellable counterattack. Think of him as the Green Liu Bei, but with a cooler mask and a penchant for hitting everyone at once.

Ekanite sports a dynamic, action-oriented look, mid-strike with a chained weapon that looks like it could hurt both the enemy and the user if not handled carefully. The green and gold color palette fits the Nature theme perfectly, and his "shinobi" attire screams agility. He's definitely one of the more "classic ninja" designs in the game, avoiding the over-the-top high-fantasy armor for something that looks like he could actually sneak up on you—or at least try to, before his chains start rattling.

Pair Ekanite with heroes who buff minions or boost health.
: These heroes can boost health or provide beefier minions, protecting Ekanite's fragile Shinobi minions behind a wall of HP.
: Fast minion summoners can create a "meat wall" that makes it harder for enemies to target Ekanite's specific counterattack minions.
Team Idea: The "Stop Hitting Yourself" Wall
Arco - Ekanite - Queen Guowang - Ogima - Bastet

: Arco provides overheal and stoneskin. Ekanite provides counterattack minions. Queen Guowang punishes enemies for using specials. Ogima clears enemy minions. It's a team designed to make the enemy regret pressing any buttons.

If you pulled Ekanite early, celebrate! He is a fantastic carry hero. He hits everyone, protects your team with minions, and is fast. Focus on his x1 charge to clear story mode waves quickly. Don't worry about his stats; for early/mid-game, he is a god.

Ekanite is a luxury tool. He's not your main war defense hero anymore. Use him in your 3rd or 4th war attack team against setups that lack minion control. He is especially fun against "Hit All" defenses (like those running Khufu or Jove), as you can pop his x1 charge and watch the enemy team suicide on your counterattack.
unless you absolutely love Ninjas or have no modern Green projects.

- Players debating his interaction with Arco's stoneskin (Yes, he reflects full damage even if he takes 1 damage!).

Ekanite is like that one cool uncle who still thinks he can breakdance. He’s got the moves (Charge speed, Counterattack), and sometimes he pulls it off beautifully, but other times he throws his back out (dies to one hit). He remains a unique tactical asset for your roster, capable of turning an enemy's strength against them. If you can keep his minions alive, he's a thorn in the side of any team. Just don't expect him to stand toe-to-toe with the gods of 2026 without some serious backup.
